name: Claude Opus 4.6
note: First Opus-class model with a 1M-token context window in beta (Claude Developer Platform only) plus Claude Code agent teams; also exposes low/medium/high/max effort levels (reported here at default high).

results.notes: Standard-scaffold figure (Anthropic's Feb 2026 system card reports 80.84%, averaged over 25 trials, max effort/adaptive thinking); Anthropic's own launch post cites a separate 81.42% obtained 'with a prompt modification' — a different methodology, not this headline number.

Terminal-Bench 2.0; per Anthropic's Feb 2026 system card this figure was measured at max effort with adaptive thinking, not the model's 'default high' setting — corrected from the original 'default (high) effort' label.

Standard single-agent figure per the dedicated Opus 4.6 benchmarks article (direct fetch). Anthropic's own launch page (anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-6) separately states 86.8% 'with a multi-agent harness' — a different configuration, not this headline number. A later retrospective Vellum article (claude-opus-4-7-benchmarks-explained) lists Opus 4.6 at 83.7% for the same benchmark; kept the dedicated article's 84.0% as the primary figure.
Confirmed via direct fetch; also corroborated by ARC Prize's own coverage citing 68.8% as new SOTA at launch.
Labeled 'OSWorld' in the vendor-relayed comparison table but is OSWorld-Verified — score range and other search corroboration ('Claude Opus 4.6 achieved 72.7% on OSWorld-Verified') confirm this.
Cross-confirmed at 0.993 on the independent llm-stats.com Tau2-Telecom leaderboard.
Also stated directly by Artificial Analysis on X: 'it reached an Elo of 1606 with adaptive thinking' — AA ran this benchmark in partnership with Anthropic ahead of launch.
No-tools configuration; with-tools scored 77.3% per the same source.
No-tools configuration, cited as the prior-generation (Opus 4.6) comparison point in Opus 4.7's own launch benchmarks article; with-tools scored 84.7% per the same source.
Multilingual MMLU, consistent with how this corpus already files Opus 4.7's 91.5% under the same slug.

AIME 2025, Section 2.12 of Anthropic's official Claude Opus 4.6 System Card (Feb 2026): 'Claude Opus 4.6 achieved a score of 99.79% without tools,' averaged over 5 trials with adaptive thinking/max effort. The system card itself flags contamination concerns may have inflated this score (per Section 2.2 of the Opus 4.5 System Card). Not previously tracked for this model; the 99.8% figure is also independently relayed by benchlm.ai. [score corrected during round-6 completeness verify: proposed 99.79 -> 99.8]

Section 2.17 of the official Claude Opus 4.6 System Card: pass@1 targeted-vulnerability-reproduction score aggregated over the 1,507-task CyberGym suite, 'Claude Opus 4.6 achieved a score of 66.6%, improving on Claude Opus 4.5's score of 51.0% and Sonnet 4.5's score of 29.8%.' No-thinking/default-effort setting with an interleaved 'think' tool. Not previously tracked for this model.

Section 2.9 of the official Claude Opus 4.6 System Card: 'The ARC Prize Foundation reports that Claude Opus 4.6 achieved 94.00% on ARC-AGI-1 ... with 120k thinking tokens and High effort on their private dataset. This is state-of-the-art.' Distinct from the already-tracked arc-agi-2 result (68.8, sourced from vellum.ai; the system card's own arc-agi-2 figure is 69.17%, essentially the same score via a different rounding/source -- not re-added as a duplicate). Independently corroborated by ARC Prize Foundation's own reporting (relayed via llm-stats.com on X).

verificationNotes: Confirmed real and current (today is 2026-07-12): anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-6 and Anthropic's Feb 2026 system card verify the 2026-02-05 release date. Corrected the note's claim of '1M-token context at GA' to 'in beta' — Anthropic's own page states the 1M window is 'in beta on the Claude Developer Platform only,' not GA. GPQA (91.3), HLE-without-tools (40.0), and MRCR-v2 1M (76%) were confirmed as literal text on their cited pages. Swapped the sourceUrl for the swe-bench and terminal-bench-2 rows away from anthropic.com (whose blog shows 81.42% for SWE-bench and no literal terminal-bench percentage) to vellum.ai's benchmark breakdown, which literally states both 80.8% and 65.4%. Also corrected the terminal-bench-2 row's effort-level description: per the system card the 65.4% figure was measured at max effort with adaptive thinking, not 'default (high) effort' as originally labeled.
